# EDGAR-X sample memos

**What this is**: research memos generated by the EDGAR-X Layer-4 agent pipeline — three Claude Fable 5 specialist agents (filing extraction, year-over-year comparison, model-signal interpretation) synthesized by a Fable 5 orchestrator — from real SEC EDGAR filings and XBRL fundamentals in Snowflake. Every memo is grounded with code-supplied provenance (Snowflake table + accession-number attribution the LLM cannot invent) and independently scored by a Claude Opus 4.8 judge against the primary sources. Scores below are reported as judged — nothing hidden or inflated.

**Coverage**: 5 companies across 3 GICS sectors. **Spend**: $11.19 on the memos and judging below (memos $8.71 + judges $2.47), plus ~$5.37 on failed/superseded attempts (output-cap truncations and two API-credit outages mid-batch), reported for completeness.

| company | sector | FY | grounding | consistency | honesty | specificity | warnings | memo cost | judge cost |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [AAPL](AAPL.md) | Information Technology | 2025 | 4 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 1 | $1.24 | $0.40 |
| [MSFT](MSFT.md) | Information Technology | 2025 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 3 | $0.58 | $0.10 |
| [NVDA](NVDA.md) | Information Technology | 2026 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 1 | $2.16 | $0.63 |
| [JNJ](JNJ.md) | Health Care | 2025 | 4 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 3 | $2.04 | $0.55 |
| [CAT](CAT.md) | Industrials | 2025 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 3 | $2.70 | $0.79 |

**Warnings** are code-verified flags (traceability of orchestrator findings to specialist outputs; metric values cross-checked against real data) — printed inside each memo, never scrubbed. Per-dimension judge justifications and concrete defects live in each `{ticker}_judge_report.md`.

## Honest gaps

- AMZN, GOOG, PG, COP, LIN were attempted but not completed: the first batch hit output-cap truncations (since fixed — NVDA's 5/5/5/5 memo was generated after the fix) and two runs were cut short by Anthropic API credit exhaustion. AMZN has ~$1.43 of sunk partial-pipeline spend and no memo.
- Sector variety is narrower than planned (3 sectors, not 8) for the same reason.
- Every memo carries code-owned limitations: the XGBoost signal is a ranked screen (test AUC 0.726), the universe is the current S&P 500 excluding Financials (survivorship-biased), and all inputs are as of the filing date.
